name: Summer Circus Spectacular
hours_label: Shows 11 AM, 2 PM & 5 PM — check schedule
blurb: The Circus Arts Conservatory and The Ringling team up for an hour of world-class circus inside the air-conditioned Historic Asolo Theater — aerialists, jugglers, acrobats and comedy. A beloved Sarasota summer tradition; about $20 adults, $15 kids 12 & under.

name: Celebrate America 250 — Sarasota History Day
blurb: Sarasota County's History Center marks America's 250th with a full day at Selby Library: a Sarasota-meets-U.S.-history timeline exhibit, a collaborative community art project, local preservation groups, plus free refreshments, crafts and hands-on activities for all ages. Featured author Doug Alderson presents two programs on Florida culture and identity.

name: Sarasota Farmers Market
address: Lemon Ave & Main St, Sarasota, FL 34236
blurb: Downtown's Saturday-morning staple since 1979 — more than 70 vendors with Gulf-coast produce, fresh seafood, flowers, baked goods, coffee and live music along Lemon Avenue. Rain or shine, dog-friendly, and the best people-watching in town before a beach afternoon.

name: Siesta Key Drum Circle
venue: Siesta Beach — south of the main pavilion
hours_label: ~7 PM until after sunset (≈8:25 PM) · Free
blurb: One of the largest drum circles in the country forms on the quartz sand every Sunday about an hour before sunset, between lifeguard stands 3 and 4. Drummers, dancers, the occasional fire spinner — bring a chair, arrive early for parking, and stay for the sunset.
photo_credit: Photo: Must Do Visitor Guides — MustDo.com
